ks1.objective: Throw your tennis ball at the mega ball to push it towards the other team's end line.
successCriteria: Stays behind own end line to throw
Goes to collect a ball without bumping into team-mates

lks2.objective: Develop throwing power and quick retrieval — keep the mega ball moving towards the opponent's line.
successCriteria: Throws hit the mega ball regularly
Retrieves a ball and returns to throwing line quickly
Adjusts power based on where the mega ball sits

uks2.objective: Combine throwing accuracy with team coordination — choose over- or under-arm based on the mega ball's position.
successCriteria: Aims at the side of the mega ball away from own end line
Coordinates with teammates on combined throws
Picks throw type deliberately based on distance

equipment.item: Cones for end lines and throwing lines

space.description: Rectangular court with two end lines. Mega ball starts in the middle. Children must throw from behind their own end line.

steps: Split the class into two teams.
Every child has a tennis ball — throw it at the mega ball.
The aim of the game is to get the mega ball to cross the opponents' end line.
Children can go anywhere to collect the balls but must throw from the end of their own playing area.
Game ends when the mega ball crosses one end line — that team wins.
questions: What strategies can you come up with as a team?
Where should you aim your throw to make the mega ball move the most?
What determines whether you should throw over or under arm?

step.space: Move the end zones further apart or closer
step.task: Children can only roll the ball at the mega ball
step.people: Play 4 ways with 4 teams (north vs south, east vs west)

cues.phrase: "Use your legs, not just your arm"

errors.cause: Aiming AT the ball not under it

errors.fix: Pause; ask 'how can six of you throw together?'
Throw doesn't count; mark the line clearly
